prompt: Which company made the Game Boy handheld console?

What is the name of the princess Mario is usually trying to rescue?
Which of these games features a character named Master Chief as its hero?
Sonic the Hedgehog was created as a mascot to rival Mario — but which Sega console did he debut on in 1991?
The original Donkey Kong arcade game (1981) featured a hero named 'Jumpman'. What did he later become famous as?
Which battle royale game drops exactly 100 players onto an island and popularised the phrase 'Winner Winner Chicken Dinner'?
The PlayStation 1 was originally developed in a partnership deal with which other company — before Nintendo pulled out at the last moment?
Donkey Kong was born because Miyamoto couldn't get the licence he originally wanted. Which cartoon franchise was the game originally going to be based on?
What was the name of the most notorious flop of the 1983 video-game crash — an Atari 2600 cartridge so badly received that millions of unsold copies were reportedly buried in a New Mexico landfill?
Which studio developed the original BioShock (2007), set in the underwater city of Rapture? The studio was known by two different names over its lifetime.
Doom (1993) revolutionised the FPS genre and was infamously self-published by its developer after cutting ties with their previous publisher. What is that studio called?
In Minecraft, which green exploding creature sneaks up behind players and hisses before detonating?
Which puzzle game has you rotating and stacking falling four-square blocks to clear horizontal lines?
In The Legend of Zelda series, what is the name of the green-clad hero the player controls?
Which mobile game launched in 2016 sent players walking around the real world to catch creatures using their phones?
In Super Mario games, what does Mario typically eat to grow from small to big?
What is Mario's day job, according to Nintendo?
In the Angry Birds games, what are the birds usually trying to attack?
Minecraft became the best-selling video game of all time. Which studio originally created it before Microsoft bought it?
The 'Konami Code' (up, up, down, down, left, right, left, right, B, A) first appeared in which 1986 NES game?
The phrase 'The cake is a lie' became a famous gaming meme thanks to which 2007 puzzle game?
In fighting games, what is the common name for the Street Fighter input where you roll the stick and press punch to throw a fireball?
Which company developed and distributes Steam, the dominant PC game storefront?
In Among Us, what is the basic goal of the players who are NOT impostors?
options: Complete tasks and vote out impostors

prompt: Released in 1972 by Atari, which game is widely credited as the first commercially successful video game?
The cooperative game Journey, praised for its wordless online encounters with strangers, was made by which studio?
Speedrunners of Super Mario Bros. chase a famous benchmark of beating the whole game in under how many minutes?
Which composer wrote the iconic orchestral and choral score for the Halo series, including its monk-style chant theme?
The roguelike deck-builder that swept the indie scene, having you climb a spire by drafting cards, is called what?
In the original arcade Pac-Man, reaching level 256 triggers a famous game-breaking bug that scrambles half the screen. Why does it happen?
In Grand Theft Auto, what does the abbreviation 'GTA' stand for?
Which long-running Nintendo series stars a pink, round creature who can swallow enemies to copy their powers?
What colour is the iconic plumber Luigi's hat and overalls trim, distinguishing him from his red-clad brother Mario?
In The Sims, what is the gibberish made-up language the characters speak called?
Which game franchise features plastic instrument controllers so you can strum along to rock songs?
In Animal Crossing, which money-grubbing raccoon runs the shop and charges you for your house?
Which Nintendo racing series has you flinging banana peels and blue shells at rival drivers?
Nintendo's mascot Mario was reportedly named after a real person. Who?
options: An Italian chef near the Kyoto office
The warehouse landlord who rented to Nintendo

prompt: Which 1998 game is frequently cited by critics and developers as one of the greatest ever made, starring Link and the Ocarina?
options: Zelda: A Link to the Past

prompt: In Counter-Strike, planting and detonating the bomb at a bombsite is the objective for which side?
The first pair of Pokemon games launched in Japan in 1996 as Red and which other colour — the one swapped for Blue when the games came West?
In Dark Souls, what short, ominous message appears on screen each time your character is killed?
Which mascot platformer character, a purple dragon, was a flagship PlayStation series in the late 1990s?
Hideo Kojima is the celebrated creator of which stealth-espionage series featuring Solid Snake?
The Witcher games are based on a book series by an author from which country?
According to its creator, the series Final Fantasy got its name because the first game was meant to be his what?
options: Final game before quitting the industry

prompt: In 1980, Atari secretly let programmer Warren Robinett hide his name inside the game Adventure. This is widely regarded as gaming's first what?
The card game phenomenon Hearthstone is set in the universe of which other Blizzard franchise?
Shigeru Miyamoto has said the design of Pikmin was partly inspired by watching what in his own garden?
The indie hit Stardew Valley was famously developed almost entirely by a single person over about four years. Who?
In Fortnite, what do players quickly construct during a firefight to gain cover and a height advantage?
Which best-selling console did Nintendo launch in 2006, where players swung a motion-sensing remote to play tennis and bowling?
Blizzard's colourful team shooter, home to heroes like Tracer, Reinhardt and Mercy, is called what?
The wildly popular game Rocket League is essentially football (soccer) played with what instead of players?
The globally acclaimed S.T.A.L.K.E.R. games, set in the irradiated Chornobyl Exclusion Zone, are made by GSC Game World — a studio based in which country?
Which 2023 role-playing game, based on Dungeons & Dragons, swept Game of the Year awards and let players talk or sneak past nearly any fight?
The hugely popular MOBA League of Legends is developed by which studio?
In the survival horror series Resident Evil, what is the sinister corporation whose bioweapon experiments cause most of the outbreaks?
Elden Ring's sprawling world was co-created by which fantasy novelist, brought in by FromSoftware to write its underlying mythology?
The original Prince of Persia (1989) got its lifelike movement because its lone creator rotoscoped footage of his own brother running. Who was that developer?
